result:
Confirmed: 35 functions in sae.c. Full analysis complete.

## CONFIG_SAE — functions whose compiled behavior depends on it (src/ tree)

**Mechanism**: two independent gates — (1) `wpa_supplicant/Makefile:241-244` only adds `src/common/sae.o` to `OBJS` when `CONFIG_SAE` is defined (whole-file gating, no in-file `#ifdef` needed), and (2) 15 literal `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` blocks scattered across 8 other src/ files (32 grep hits total, all accounted for).

### "whole" — function exists only under CONFIG_SAE (49 functions)

**src/common/sae.c** (entire file only compiled when `CONFIG_SAE` — all 35 functions, none individually `#ifdef`'d since the whole file is build-gated):
`sae_set_group`, `sae_clear_temp_data`, `sae_clear_data`, `buf_shift_right`, `sae_get_rand`, `sae_get_rand_and_mask`, `sae_pwd_seed_key`, `get_rand_1_to_p_1`, `is_quadratic_residue_blind`, `sae_test_pwd_seed_ecc`, `sae_test_pwd_seed_ffc`, `get_random_qr_qnr`, `sae_derive_pwe_ecc`, `sae_derive_pwe_ffc`, `sae_derive_commit_element_ecc`, `sae_derive_commit_element_ffc`, `sae_derive_commit`, `sae_prepare_commit`, `sae_derive_k_ecc`, `sae_derive_k_ffc`, `sae_derive_keys`, `sae_process_commit`, `sae_write_commit`, `sae_group_allowed`, `sae_parse_commit_token`, `sae_parse_commit_scalar`, `sae_parse_commit_element_ecc`, `sae_parse_commit_element_ffc`, `sae_parse_commit_element`, `sae_parse_commit`, `sae_cn_confirm`, `sae_cn_confirm_ecc`, `sae_cn_confirm_ffc`, `sae_write_confirm`, `sae_check_confirm`.

**src/ap/ieee802_11.c**, all defined entirely inside the `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE … #endif` block at lines 318–876 (14):
`auth_build_sae_commit`, `auth_build_sae_confirm`, `auth_sae_send_commit`, `auth_sae_send_confirm`, `use_sae_anti_clogging`, `check_sae_token`, `auth_build_token_req`, `sae_check_big_sync`, `auth_sae_retransmit_timer`, `sae_clear_retransmit_timer` (real body), `sae_set_retransmit_timer`, `sae_sm_step`, `handle_auth_sae`, `auth_sae_init_committed`.

Note: `sae_clear_retransmit_timer` is a special case — under `CONFIG_SAE` it's the real function above; when `CONFIG_SAE` is *not* defined, `src/ap/ieee802_11.h:101-104` supplies a `static inline` no-op stub instead. Either way its only real implementation is CONFIG_SAE-only, so it's "whole."

### "partial" — function always exists, only part of its body is conditional (9 functions)

| Function | File | Conditional lines | What's gated |
|---|---|---|---|
| `handle_auth` | src/ap/ieee802_11.c | 954-957, 1180-1200 | one `||` clause of an auth-alg check; the `WLAN_AUTH_SAE` switch `case` |
| `check_assoc_ies` | src/ap/ieee802_11.c | 1538-1560 | PMKSA-caching / AKM validation for SAE-authenticated STAs |
| `ap_free_sta` | src/ap/sta_info.c | 313-316 | frees `sta->sae` |
| `wpa_write_rsn_ie` | src/ap/wpa_auth_ie.c | 191-202 | adds `RSN_AUTH_KEY_MGMT_SAE`/`FT_SAE` selectors to the advertised AKM suite list |
| `wpa_validate_wpa_ie` | src/ap/wpa_auth_ie.c | 524-529, 606-611 | SAE AKM selector mapping (two separate spots in the same function) |
| `hostapd_wpa_auth_get_psk` | src/ap/wpa_auth_glue.c | 231-237 | returns `sta->sae->pmk` for SAE-authenticated STAs |
| `hostapd_ctrl_iface_sta_mib` | src/ap/ctrl_iface_ap.c | 148-155 | appends `sae_group=` to the STA MIB dump |
| `rsn_key_mgmt_to_bitfield` | src/common/wpa_common.c | 485-490 | maps `RSN_AUTH_KEY_MGMT_SAE`/`FT_SAE` selectors to `WPA_KEY_MGMT_*` bits |
| `wpa_gen_wpa_ie_rsn` | src/rsn_supp/wpa_ie.c | 173-178 | emits the SAE AKM selector when writing the RSN IE |

### Excluded (checked, not counted)

- **Struct-field-only blocks** — `src/ap/hostapd.h:268-273` (`sae_token_key` etc. in `struct hostapd_data`) and `src/ap/sta_info.h:162-164` (`struct sae_data *sae` in `struct sta_info`) declare data members, not functions. They don't gate any function's *code*, only which functions above can legally touch those fields (already captured in the partial list).
- **`wpa_key_mgmt_sae()`, `wpa_auth_uses_sae()`, `wpa_auth_uses_ft_sae()`** (`src/common/defs.h`, `src/ap/wpa_auth.c`) — always compiled, no `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` anywhere near them; they just test bitmask flags. Not affected by the macro at compile time, so excluded despite being SAE-related and called from CONFIG_SAE blocks.
- `src/common/Makefile:14` (`CFLAGS += -DCONFIG_SAE`) — unconditional define for a separate lib target, not a gate.
- `src/ap/ieee802_11.h:97-105` — not a separate item; it's the declaration side of `sae_clear_retransmit_timer`, folded into that entry above.

**Total: 58 functions depend on CONFIG_SAE — 49 "whole", 9 "partial".**
